channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Gunman shoots several tourists at historic pyramids in Mexico
item.description: A man standing atop one of the historic Teotihuacan pyramids opened fire on tourists Monday, killing one Canadian and leaving at least 13 people, authorities said.
item.content:encoded:
A man standing atop one of the historic Teotihuacan pyramids opened fire on tourists Monday, killing one Canadian and leaving at least 13 people, authorities said.

(Image credit: Eduardo Verdugo)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Cuba confirms meeting with US officials on island, wants energy blockade lifted
item.description: Cuba's government confirmed that it had recently met with U.S. officials on the island as tensions between the two sides remain high over the U.S. energy blockade of the Caribbean country.
item.content:encoded:
Cuba's government confirmed that it had recently met with U.S. officials on the island as tensions between the two sides remain high over the U.S. energy blockade of the Caribbean country.

(Image credit: Ramon Espinosa)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Japan approves scrapping a ban on lethal weapons exports
item.description: The approval clears a final set of hurdles for Japan's postwar arms sales and facilitate its future sale of weapons such as a next-generation fighter jet and combat drones.
item.content:encoded:
The approval clears a final set of hurdles for Japan's postwar arms sales and facilitate its future sale of weapons such as a next-generation fighter jet and combat drones.

(Image credit: Keisuke Hosojima/AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Here are the results for Virginia's 2026 redistricting ballot measure
item.description: Live election results: Get the latest on Virginia's special election on redistricting.
item.content:encoded: Live election results: Get the latest on Virginia's special election on redistricting.
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: The Onion has agreed to a new deal to take over Infowars
item.description: The Onion says it has a new deal to take over conspiracy theorist Alex Jones's Infowars media company. If approved, the satirical news website could turn Infowars into a parody of itself.
item.content:encoded:
The Onion says it has a new deal to take over conspiracy theorist Alex Jones's Infowars media company. If approved, the satirical news website could turn Infowars into a parody of itself.

(Image credit: Mario Tama)
item.dc:creator: Tovia Smith

item.title: Tim Cook to step down as Apple CEO. In letter, describes 15 years of emails
item.description: The 65-year-old Cook will turn over CEO duties to Apple's head of hardware products, John Ternus, in September. Cook will remain with the company as executive chairman.
item.content:encoded:
The 65-year-old Cook will turn over CEO duties to Apple's head of hardware products, John Ternus, in September. Cook will remain with the company as executive chairman.

(Image credit: Godofredo A. Vásquez)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: Trump's labor secretary resigns amid investigation into misconduct
item.description: Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer, who was under internal investigation, is leaving her position. She becomes the third cabinet departure of President Trump's second term.
item.content:encoded:
Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer, who was under internal investigation, is leaving her position. She becomes the third cabinet departure of President Trump's second term.

(Image credit: Drew Angerer)
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu

item.title: The 11 most challenged books of 2025, according to the American Library Association
item.description: The ALA says 4,235 titles were challenged at U.S. libraries — the second-highest year on record. Forty percent of the challenged works involved LGBTQ+ subjects or the experiences of people of color.
item.content:encoded:
Sold by Patricia McCormick, The Perks of Being a Wallflower by Stephen Chbosky and Maia Kobabe's Gender Queer: A Memoir.'/>

The ALA says 4,235 titles were challenged at U.S. libraries — the second-highest year on record. Forty percent of the challenged works involved LGBTQ+ subjects or the experiences of people of color.

(Image credit: American Library Association)
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas

item.title: Peace talks are in doubt as the U.S. seizes an Iranian ship
item.description: President Trump said a U.S. delegation will head to Pakistan to resume talks to end the war with Iran, but Tehran expressed reluctance after the U.S. seized one of its cargo ships in the Strait of Hormuz.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump said a U.S. delegation will head to Pakistan to resume talks to end the war with Iran, but Tehran expressed reluctance after the U.S. seized one of its cargo ships in the Strait of Hormuz.

(Image credit: Atta Kenare)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: A mine despoiled the beauty of the rainforest. This Goldman Prize winner took action
item.description: "We women are the land guardians and keepers," says Theonila Roka Matbob of Papua New Guinea, recognized for her efforts to repair the environmental and social harms caused by a copper and gold mine.
item.content:encoded:
"We women are the land guardians and keepers," says Theonila Roka Matbob of Papua New Guinea, recognized for her efforts to repair the environmental and social harms caused by a copper and gold mine.

(Image credit: Goldman Environmental Prize)
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Emanuel
